---
title: "Simulation"
subtitle: "Generate clean BOLD responses, noise, and matrix time series."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Clean Condition Signals

`simulate_bold_signal()` creates condition-wise HRF-convolved responses. The
first column of `mat` is time; the remaining columns are condition signals.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import fmrimod as fm

sim = fm.simulate_bold_signal(
    ncond=3,
    amps=[1.0, 1.5, 2.0],
    tr=2.0,
    seed=10,
)

signals = pd.DataFrame(
    sim["mat"],
    columns=["time", "cond_1", "cond_2", "cond_3"],
)
signals.head()
```

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5
ax = signals.plot(x="time", y=["cond_1", "cond_2", "cond_3"])
ax.set_xlabel("Time (s)")
ax.set_ylabel("Response")
plt.show()
```

## Noise Components

`simulate_noise_vector()` combines temporal correlation, low-frequency drift,
and optional physiological sinusoids.

```{python}
n_timepoints = 200
tr = 2.0
time = np.arange(n_timepoints) * tr

whiteish = fm.simulate_noise_vector(
    n_timepoints,
    tr=tr,
    ar=[],
    ma=[],
    drift_amplitude=0.0,
    physio=False,
    seed=1,
)
structured = fm.simulate_noise_vector(n_timepoints, tr=tr, seed=1)

pd.DataFrame({
    "whiteish_sd": [whiteish.std()],
    "structured_sd": [structured.std()],
}).round(3)
```

```{python}
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.5
noise_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "time": time,
    "whiteish": whiteish,
    "structured": structured,
})
ax = noise_df.plot(x="time", y=["whiteish", "structured"], alpha=0.85)
ax.set_xlabel("Time (s)")
ax.set_ylabel("Noise")
plt.show()
```

## Signal Plus Noise

`simulate_simple_dataset()` returns clean and noisy matrices for small method
checks.

```{python}
dataset = fm.simulate_simple_dataset(ncond=3, tr=2.0, snr=0.5, seed=2)

dataset["clean"].shape, dataset["noisy"].shape
```

## Matrix Time Series

`simulate_fmri_matrix()` varies event amplitude and duration across columns,
mirroring the R vignette's column-specific simulation examples.

```{python}
matrix_sim = fm.simulate_fmri_matrix(
    n=5,
    total_time=120,
    TR=2.0,
    n_events=8,
    durations=0.5,
    amplitude_sd=0.2,
    duration_sd=0.1,
    noise_type="ar1",
    random_seed=3,
)

ts = matrix_sim["time_series"].get_all_data()
ts.shape, matrix_sim["ampmat"].shape, matrix_sim["durmat"].shape
```

## Next

- [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd)
- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
